pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 14) and export_result.sh (line 10) - consistent path specified in all task scripts

Standard PDF MIME type confirmed by RFC 3778; Floreant POS exports reports as PDF files (confirmed by web search of Floreant POS 1.4 documentation); export_result.sh uses 'file --mime-type' command which returns 'application/pdf' for valid PDF files

PDF format includes mandatory header ('%PDF-1.x'), objects, streams, xref table, and trailer; a simple report with headers and minimal data typically exceeds 1000 bytes; verifier.py line 72 enforces threshold > 1000 bytes; similar export_sales_report task uses 500 bytes as minimum, indicating this is a reasonable and conservative threshold

privileged_info_summary: Floreant POS 1.4 build 1707b is a real, open-source restaurant point-of-sale system available on SourceForge that includes menu usage reporting capabilities. The task requires exporting a Menu Usage report as a PDF file to /home/ga/Desktop/menu_usage.pdf. PDF files exported by Floreant POS conform to the application/pdf MIME type standard and contain sufficient PDF structure (headers, objects, metadata) to exceed 1000 bytes minimum file size. The environment uses a pre-populated Apache Derby database with sample restaurant data, making the data real rather than synthetic.
